classification_notes: Altair is derived from the calligraphic Digitalino typeface, giving it humanist roots with organic stroke contrast that distinguishes it from geometric or grotesque sans-serif traditions.
summary: A seven-weight humanist sans with calligraphic roots, derived from Zetafonts' own Digitalino and suited to corporate and editorial work.
description: Expanded from Francesco Canovaro's original bold design for Digitalino, Altair runs from Thin to Ultra across 14 styles including matching italics. Its calligraphic underpinning gives the letterforms a warmth that suits brand voices and editorial contexts where a neutral grotesque would feel too cold. Coverage extends to 202 languages across Latin and Greek scripts.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Altair as a sans serif family derived from their Digitalino typeface, with the original bold design expanded to seven weights for use across body copy and display text. They highlight its calligraphic roots as giving it a friendly, confident character suited to corporate voices, technology startups and news contexts where solid design needs emotional warmth.

name: Antipasto Pro
classification_notes: Zetafonts explicitly describes Antipasto as a geometric sans serif; the circular, constructed letterforms and uniform stroke weight confirm this classification.
summary: A nine-weight geometric sans with Latin, Greek and Cyrillic coverage, expanded and refined in 2017 from an original three-weight release.
description: Originally a three-weight geometric sans by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, the family was revised and extended in 2017 as Antipasto Pro, adding six weights from Hairline to Extrabold and broadening script coverage to Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 205 languages. OpenType features include small caps, old-style numerals, ordinals and discretionary ligatures, giving the family genuine typographic depth for editorial and branding use. A companion Icons subfamily mirrors each of the nine weights with a matching symbol set.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Antipasto Pro as a revision and expansion of the original Antipasto family, designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Francesco Canovaro and released in 2017 as part of the Rounded set from Arista. The Pro version adds Cyrillic and Greek characters, six new weights and OpenType features including small caps and old-style numerals, while backwards compatibility with the original fonts is maintained via included compatibility packs.

name: Another Shabby
classification_notes: A handwritten casual script defined by rough, wide brushstrokes with slightly rounded corners, placing it firmly in the casual brush script subcategory. The textured wet and raw variants add watercolour and chalk effects layered over the base script forms.
summary: A rough brush script family by Francesco Canovaro with a watercolour-textured variant covering Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 205 languages.
description: Built from wide, slightly rounded brushstrokes that simulate casual handwriting, the family runs across four weights from Light to Heavy. Another Wet Shabby extends the family with per-character watercolour texturing for multilayered colour compositions without post-production. Coverage spans 575 glyphs across 205 languages in Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts, though the textured variant is processor-intensive and unsuitable for long text blocks.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Another Shabby as a script family whose shapes are defined by rough, wide brushstrokes, slightly rounded at the corners to evoke real handwritten casual lettering. The Raw and Wet versions are designed for watercolour-textured and chalk-effect multilayered colour images without photo-retouch software, and the family supports over 200 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic alphabets.

name: Florentia
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a humanist sans-serif, drawing from Roman monumental capitals and stone carvings in Florence, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition with classical proportions and a lower x-height.
summary: A humanist sans-serif by Francesco Canovaro that draws on Roman monumental lettering and Florentine stone carving, extended to cover Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 196 languages.
description: Developed from Canovaro's earlier Beatrix Family, Florentia translates the inscriptional spirit of Santa Croce Cathedral into a contemporary sans with a lower x-height and refined proportions. Nine weights from Thin to Fat each carry a matching italic, giving the family 18 styles in total. The character set spans 561 glyphs across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic, with OpenType features including ligatures, stylistic alternates, fractions, ordinals and scientific inferiors.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Florentia as a humanist sans-serif designed by Francesco Canovaro, released in 2017, built on the classical Roman capital model of the Beatrix Family and shaped by the stone carvings found in Florence's Santa Croce Cathedral. The result is a contemporary design with a lower x-height and an extended character set supporting over 40 Latin-script languages as well as Greek and Russian Cyrillic.

name: Kitten
classification_notes: Kitten is a multi-weight connected script with a hand-lettered, signpainter aesthetic, spanning upright and slanted variants with swash alternates and OpenType features typical of display scripts.
summary: A multi-weight signpainter script by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, with swash variants, OpenType ligatures and a companion set of cat dingbats.
description: Spanning six weights from Monoline to Fat, Kitten draws on the hand-lettered aesthetic of sign painting, with curvier forms in the heavier weights suited to logos and display use, and cleaner lighter cuts that work for shorter text blocks. Three style variants — Regular, Slant and Swash — extend the family to 16 styles in total, while OpenType features provide uppercase and lowercase ligatures, alternates and swash characters. Cat silhouette dingbats, designed by Isabella Ahmadzadeh, are accessed via the Dingcats subfamily. The family was recognised with a PRINT Award for typeface design in 2016.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Kitten as a multi-weight script family with a signpainter aesthetic and a wide range of variants, alternates and ligatures. The curvy bold shapes suit logo and display use, while lighter weights and the condensed Slant variant work for longer text. It covers over 40 languages using the Latin alphabet, and makes full use of OpenType ligatures to avoid letter clashes and provide a more handmade, natural look. Swashes are accessible via the glyph palette or by typing underscores after a letter.

name: Brushstrike
classification_notes: An all-caps gestural brush typeface with a strikethrough alternate set, designed explicitly for logo design and display use rather than running text.
summary: An all-caps brush display typeface by Francesco Canovaro, featuring a strikethrough alternate set aimed at logo design and word highlighting.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro and released in 2015, Brushstrike carries no lowercase characters, instead doubling the uppercase with a strikethrough alternate set accessible via OpenType features. Two weights, Light and Regular, share a consistent gestural brush aesthetic; the lighter weight is intended for use reversed out of dark backgrounds to create light effects. Coverage extends to 202 Latin-script languages across 422 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Brushstrike as an exercise in dynamic, gestural brush type design. The family omits lowercase entirely and provides a strikethrough alternate uppercase set for dynamic logo design and unusual word highlighting. Two weights are available: the light weight (also known as Lightstrike) can be paired with the heavier regular or used white-on-dark for light effects.

name: Bubbleboddy Neue
classification_notes: A chunky, round display typeface with deliberately soft letterforms evoking 1980s bubblegum aesthetics, designed primarily for logos and headline use rather than text.
summary: A redesign of one of Zetafonts' earliest typefaces, reworked with additional weights and extended script support to preserve its round, chunky 1980s character.
description: Originally one of Zetafonts' first releases, the Neue version adds Thin, Light and Bold weights alongside the existing Regular and Extrabold, plus a decorative Inline cut. The redesign also introduced Latin, Greek and Cyrillic coverage across 200 languages and improved on-screen readability. The soft, inflated letterforms suit logos, packaging and display work where a retro bubblegum personality is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Bubbleboddy Neue as a full redesign of one of their earliest typefaces, retaining its round and chunky flavour while adding three new weights and a complete Cyrillic and Greek character set. They position it as a display and logo family with an 80s touch and the sweetness of bubblegum, updated with better screen readability for both web and text-setting use.

name: Aliens and Cows
classification_notes: An ultra-condensed sans-serif designed explicitly for display use, inspired by 1980s sci-fi movie title cards. Its extreme compression and wide tracking place it firmly in the display category rather than general-purpose sans-serif.
summary: An ultra-condensed sans-serif display typeface by Francesco Canovaro, drawn from 1980s science fiction movie title cards with wide tracking and sci-fi themed alternates.
description: Seven weights from Thin to Heavy, plus a variable weight axis, give Aliens and Cows a broad tonal range despite its singular ultra-condensed form. Wide letterspacing counterpoints the narrow letterforms, lending it an airy quality suited to minimal logo work and editorial headlines. Sci-fi themed stylistic alternates, lined small caps and word ligatures round out the feature set, with coverage across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts for 198 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Aliens and Cows as an ultra-condensed sans-serif display typeface by Francesco Canovaro, released in 2016 and inspired by the title cards of 1980s science fiction films. They highlight its thin letterforms paired with ultra-wide spacing as ideal for minimal logo design and editorial display use, noting sci-fi themed alternates, lined small caps, word ligatures and coverage of over forty languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts.

name: Armonioso
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a script typeface with a soft brush quality, placing it firmly in the brush script subcategory.
summary: A single-weight brush script by Francesco Canovaro with a soft, domestic hand-lettered quality.
description: Released in 2014, this brush script carries a gentle, informal character suited to packaging, greeting design and editorial work with a personal tone. The glyph set covers 418 characters across 202 languages with broad Latin diacritic support. Its soft stroke modulation keeps it approachable without slipping into the overwrought expressiveness of many display brushes.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Armonioso as a script typeface with a soft brush homely feeling, designed by Francesco Canovaro and released in version 1.0.2 in 2014, with Latin writing system support across 202 languages.

name: Cocobiker
classification_notes: Designed as a display small caps typeface with geometric proportions, runic mood and ultraconnected geometry; it draws from contemporary grotesque conventions but is configured for display use rather than running text.
summary: A display small caps variant of the Coco Gothic family, built on geometric proportions with a runic character and interconnected letterforms.
description: Part of the wider Coco Gothic family, this typeface applies a runic mood and tightly connected geometry to a small caps structure designed primarily for display settings. Five weights run from Ultralight to Heavy, and 634 glyphs cover Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 205 languages, with OpenType support for ligatures and special symbols. The positioning towards cycling and subculture aesthetics gives it a narrow niche, though the weight range and script coverage add practical breadth.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Cocobiker as a variant of the Coco Gothic family inspired by contemporary grotesque typefaces, with a runic mood, geometric proportions and ultraconnected geometry. Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and released in 2015, it is billed as a sans serif font dedicated to hipster culture and bike lovers, designed as a display small caps typeface that remains readable in text blocks. It features an extended character set with European language support, Greek and Cyrillic alphabets, and OpenType ligatures, dingbats and symbols.

name: A Day Without Sun
classification_notes: An ultra-condensed, hand-drawn display typeface with a grungy, handmade texture and vintage handlettering aesthetic, placing it firmly in the display category rather than a conventional script or sans.
summary: An ultra-condensed, hand-drawn display typeface derived from Panforte, evoking vintage handlettered menus with a deliberately grungy texture.
description: Drawn in loose, expressive strokes by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini from Debora Manetti's Panforte design, it compresses letterforms to an extreme degree while retaining a faux-handmade quality suited to decorative titling and menu work. A Text subfamily offers Regular, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic for smaller settings where the grungy detail softens. OpenType features include small capitals, oldstyle figures, ligatures and a set of special ligature dingbats, with coverage extending across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts for over 200 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe A Day Without Sun as an ultra-condensed companion to Panforte, hand drawn in easy big strokes to evoke the subtle elegance of vintage handlettered menus. They note that its grungy, handmade character reads well at medium and large sizes without disrupting text settings, and highlight support for over forty Latin-script languages alongside Greek and Cyrillic, with OpenType features including small caps, oldstyle numerals, ligatures and special ligature dingbats.

name: Adlery
classification_notes: A handmade brush typeface designed explicitly for display use, with fluid letterforms and alternating lowercase glyphs typical of brush script styles.
summary: A brush display script by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini with three stylistic variants and alternating lowercase letters to avoid repeated glyphs.
description: Designed for display use, the family comprises three styles: Pro, Pro Blockletter and Swash. A double set of lowercase alphabets cycles automatically so that no two consecutive identical letters share the same form, giving running text a more natural, handwritten rhythm. Coverage extends across Latin and Cyrillic scripts with 639 glyphs supporting 199 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Adlery as a brush typeface by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini optimised for maximum readability at medium sizes. It ships in three styles with a double lowercase alphabet that alternates in writing, standard ligatures, auto lowercase ligatures and stylistic uppercase alternates.

name: Arista Pro
designer: Francesco Canovaro, Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: Arista Pro is described as geometric and soft in approach, with rounded terminals and a clean monoline construction characteristic of geometric sans-serif design. The rounded quality distinguishes it from strict geometric or humanist models.
summary: A rounded geometric sans with eight weights, alternate character sets and a companion icon subfamily, covering Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 205 languages.
description: Originally released as Arista Z in 2007 and reworked into this pro edition in 2017, the family carries a soft geometric construction across eight weights from Hairline to Fat. Two stylistic versions offer design flexibility, and the companion Icons subfamily uses variable-width monoline glyphs calibrated to match text weight up to SemiBold. Small capitals and oldstyle figures are included as OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Arista Pro as the definitive version of the Arista typeface, first released as Arista Z in 2007 and reissued as Arista 2.0 in 2010. The pro edition retains the geometric and soft character of the original while expanding the family to a full weight range, two alternate versions, Greek and Cyrillic support, and a companion icon typeface with variable-width monoline icons sized to match the text weights.

name: Altro
designer: Francesco Canovaro, Andrea Tartarelli, Mario De Libero
classification_notes: The primary subfamily is a Swiss-inspired, low-contrast humanist grotesque; the family also includes a flare serif variant and an old-style transitional serif, making the grotesque the anchoring category.
summary: A three-subfamily type system pairing a Swiss-inspired grotesque, a flared variant and an old-style transitional serif across a shared set of eight weights and proportions.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro with Andrea Tartarelli and Mario De Libero, Altro builds a coherent system from three distinct voices: a low-contrast humanist grotesque with a slightly low x-height, a flare serif bridge, and an old-style serif with square dots and restrained ball terminals. All three subfamilies share the same proportions and weight range from Extralight to Fat, allowing switches between styles without disrupting typographic rhythm. The full family runs to 48 styles plus two variable fonts covering weight and serif axes.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Altro as a typeface that moves between humanist grotesque and old-style serif within a single family, designed for visual harmony across different aesthetics. The Grotesk is positioned as a Swiss-inspired workhorse with a touch of elegance from its slightly lower x-height, while the Serif offers an old-style transitional design with square dots and a brutalist treatment of ball terminals. The family supports 207 languages across 638 glyphs and is available with variable font support in the full family package.

name: Tarif Arabic
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Andrea Tartarelli, Rania Azmi
classification_notes: A sans-serif with humanist proportions and inverted contrast, adapting the quirky postmodern character of the Latin Tarif into Arabic script forms.
summary: An Arabic companion to the Tarif family, translating its inverted contrast and quirky proportions into modern Arabic script across seven weights plus a variable font.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Andrea Tartarelli with Arabic collaboration from Rania Azmi, this typeface carries the idiosyncratic character of the original Tarif into Arabic letterforms. The inverted contrast and unconventional proportions that define the Latin version are reinterpreted through the fluid connections of Arabic script, resulting in a design suited to titling, logos and display use. A variable weight axis and extensive OpenType features including ligatures, alternate characters and multiple numeral sets round out the family.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Tarif Arabic as the Arabic counterpart to the original Tarif, inspired by the spirit of cultural exchange in 10th century Andalusia. They note it reinterprets the humanist structure and inverted contrast of the Latin version into modern Arabic script, with quirky proportions and minimalistic elegance suited to titling, logos and editorial use. The Arabic weights ship with a limited Latin character set, though full commercial weights include complete support for both scripts across 216 languages.

name: Hello Script
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Andrea Tartarelli, Cristiana Pezzatini
classification_notes: A high-contrast calligraphic script drawn with a parallel nib, featuring monoline swashes and strong round body shapes, placing it firmly in the calligraphic script category.
summary: A high-contrast calligraphic script with parallel-nib body shapes, monoline swashes and multilayer colour fill variants for festive and decorative use.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Hello Script uses strong round letterforms drawn with a parallel nib alongside monoline swashes and terminals. It ships with over ten swash alternates, OpenType ligatures, contextual alternates and terminal forms, plus fill and striped-fill variants intended for multilayer colour font composition. The family extends to a sans-serif companion and a Christmas-themed branch with iconographic cuts, covering 188 languages across the Latin script.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Hello Script as a high contrast calligraphic script featuring monoline swashes and strong round body shapes drawn with a parallel nib. It supports over 40 Latin-alphabet languages with a full range of accents and diacritics, and includes more than ten swash variants along with two decorative fill typefaces for use as multilayer colour fonts. The wider Hello family comprises a sans-serif companion, a Christmas-themed version and a set of Christmas icons.

name: Deep Dream
classification_notes: Deep Dream Zero is a geometric unicase sans serif with AI-inspired irregularities; Deep Dream Eighties is a bitmap unicase display face. Both are firmly display in intent and construction, with unicase logic and deliberate optical distortion.
summary: A two-subfamily display family by Zetafonts built from AI-generated specimens, pairing a geometric unicase sans with a pixel-distorted bitmap face.
description: Created for the OFFF TLV 2022 catalogue, the family comprises Deep Dream Zero, a five-weight geometric unicase sans with asymmetric counters and nonstandard letter constructions drawn from a machine-generated specimen, and Deep Dream Eighties, a single-weight unicase bitmap face derived from an AI-generated 1980s movie logo. Both subfamilies translate neural-network artifacts, such as irregular rhythm and unexpected ligatures, into a consistent typographic system rather than leaving them as raw glitches. A variable font is available for Deep Dream Zero, and the family covers 177 languages across the Latin script.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Deep Dream as a research typeface family exploring AI-assisted typography, developed using early neural network image generators including Dall-E and Midjourney. The two subfamilies share a unicase logic and AI-inspired irregularity, turning generative noise into systematic design language, and were produced as part of the Singularities set for the OFFF TLV 2022 'A Beautiful Mistake' catalogue.

name: Delizioso
classification_notes: A single-weight display sans built from circular modules and sharp verticals with high contrast, intended solely for headlines and logos rather than text setting, placing it firmly in the display category with a geometric construction approach.
summary: A single-style geometric display sans by Francesco Canovaro built on circular counters and sharp verticals for use in logos and short headlines.
description: Released in 2008, it uses large circular modules punched into heavy letterforms to create high-contrast capitals with a retro-modern character that references both vintage lettering and Bauhaus geometry. The design ships as one style only, with no italic or weight variants, making it a focused tool for posters, logotypes and short display copy. Its 225-glyph character set covers 109 languages across Latin-script European writing systems.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Delizioso as a geometric display sans by Francesco Canovaro that uses big circular modules and sharp verticals to produce heavy, high-contrast letterforms with counters punched out as perfect circles. They position it as retro-modern, nodding to both vintage lettering and Bauhaus geometry while remaining playful and bold, and say it excels in logos, attention-grabbing headlines and posters.

name: UX Sans
classification_notes: UX Sans is explicitly described as a geometric sans serif, with wide open proportions and a monowidth system across all weights, characteristic of geometric sans design applied to interface contexts.
summary: A geometric sans designed specifically for web interfaces, built around a monowidth proportion system that keeps character widths consistent across all seven weights.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro from two decades of UX and interface design practice, UX Sans prioritises legibility at every weight through wide proportions, generous spacing and disambiguation details such as a tailed lowercase 'l' and serif uppercase 'I'. The monowidth system means changing text weight on rollover does not disrupt layout, a practical concern central to the design. The family spans 42 styles across Roman, Stereo and Mono subfamilies, with a variable font and a built-in icon set for visual consistency across platforms.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe UX Sans as a highly readable geometric sans tailored for web interfaces, developed by Francesco Canovaro out of over 20 years of web and interface design experience. The family includes a monowidth proportion system to prevent layout disruption during weight changes, an embedded icon set in multiple weights for cross-platform consistency, and a glyph set of over 800 characters covering more than 218 languages across Latin and Cyrillic scripts.

name: Milligram
classification_notes: A grotesque sans-serif that draws on metal-type quirks such as slanted terminal cuts and tapered verticals rather than strict Swiss modernism, placing it firmly in the humanised grotesque tradition.
summary: A 102-style grotesque super-family by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Andrea Tartarelli that borrows metal-type warmth to soften its modernist structure across display, text and width subfamilies.
description: Built across nine subfamilies spanning five widths and up to seven weights each, the family separates display and text spacing into dedicated cuts so each application gets appropriate aperture and tracking. The Macro subfamily pushes glyphs close together for dense, sculptural wordmarks and titling, while Milligram Text opens out for body copy and UI use. Slanted terminal cuts, slightly tapered verticals and soft curve joins give the grotesque structure a warmer reading texture without departing from its modernist proportions. Four variable font files covering roman and italic in both standard and Macro styles complete the release.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Milligram as a grotesque that follows the sans-serif canon without pursuing clinical Swiss orthodoxy, borrowing from metal-type quirks to achieve a warmer, more human reading texture. The family is built for real-world typography: the main family is oriented to display use, Milligram Text loosens spacing for paragraphs and UI copy, and the Macro subfamily turns modernist economy into a postmodern aesthetic with tight spacing for dense wordmarks. Width options from Narrow to Compressed allow a consistent voice across web, product and environmental contexts, and the full family includes 102 styles plus four variable font files.

name: Geppetto
classification_notes: A revival of a condensed Tuscan wood type family from the 1903 Tubbs catalog, featuring the bifurcated serifs and ornamental character typical of 19th-century display wood type. Classified as display due to its clear decorative, large-scale heritage.
summary: A variable typeface revival of a condensed Tuscan wood type from the 1903 Tubbs catalog, expanding across a width axis named after the carpenter who made Pinocchio.
description: Rooted in a design probably originating with William Hamilton Page in 1859, Geppetto revives a condensed Tuscan letterform as a variable typeface that stretches along a width axis. The four named instances, True, Small Lie, Medium Lie and Big Lie, frame the project as a meditation on authenticity and what is preserved or lost when historical wood type is translated into digital form. It covers 152 languages across Latin and Greek scripts and includes discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and four stylistic sets.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Geppetto as both a love letter to wood type letterforms and an exploration of authenticity in typeface revival. The project began as a revival of a condensed Tuscan family from the 1903 Tubbs Wood Type catalog, likely derived from an 1859 design by William Hamilton Page, and asks which version of a typeface is the real one and which is merely a ghost.

name: Quenta
classification_notes: A condensed serif display face with layered shadow styles derived from early twentieth-century showcard lettering traditions; the multiple colour-font layers and decorative register place it firmly in the display category rather than text.
summary: A condensed display serif in four layered styles, drawn from early twentieth-century Australian showcard lettering and the handlettered masthead of the Queensland weekly magazine.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro with Debora Manetti and released in 2022 as part of the Singularities set, Quenta takes its proportions and decorative sensibility from the handlettered masthead of Queenslander magazine and the showcard manuals of penmasters such as Ross F. George and Samuel Weld. The four styles — Regular, Headline, Shadowed and Layer Shadow — are designed to stack as colour-font layers, reproducing the highlight effects typical of mid-century advertising lettering. Discretionary ligatures introduce script elements that extend the family's range for editorial headers and logo work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Quenta as inspired by the handlettered masthead of Queenslander, a nineteenth-century Australian weekly magazine covering society, sport and rural life. Designer Francesco Canovaro researched showcard lettering manuals by penmasters such as Ross F. George to find the peculiar ambiguity between calligraphic execution and contour drawing that gives Quenta its warm, vintage character. The name itself is drawn from J.R.R. Tolkien's Silmarillion, where 'Quenta' is the Elvish word for 'story'. The family ships in four styles with colour-font formats and supports 201 languages across the Latin script.

name: Automat Grotesk
classification_notes: Explicitly positioned within the European modernist grotesque tradition, drawing on Akzidenz-Grotesk, Helvetica and Univers as reference points. Open proportions, restrained contrast and regulated rhythm place it firmly in the rational grotesque subcategory.
summary: A 2025 grotesque by Francesco Canovaro built on the European modernist tradition, extended into monowidth and monospaced variants for technical and interface use.
description: Drawn in seven weights with italics across proportional, monowidth and monospaced subfamilies, it covers 218 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts. The design prioritises open proportions and character differentiation to maintain legibility at both text and display sizes, while avoiding the rigidity typical of strict rationalist grotesques. Variable font support is included in the full family package, and a broad OpenType feature set covers stylistic alternates, old-style figures, fractions and case-sensitive forms.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Automat Grotesk as a contemporary grotesque grounded in the European modernist tradition, informed by Akzidenz-Grotesk, Helvetica, Univers and Berthold. They highlight its open proportions, restrained contrast and regulated rhythm as supporting legibility across sizes, and position the monowidth and monospaced variants as tools for technical typography, coding environments and UX/UI design requiring minimalism and long-term reliability.

name: Modulo3
classification_notes: Built on a three-part grid, Modulo3 sits firmly in the display category as a geometric modular typeface; its construction prioritises pattern-making and visual texture over conventional legibility, placing it well outside standard text or sans-serif use.
summary: A squared, grid-based display typeface that pushes letterforms towards pattern and texture rather than straightforward legibility.
description: Built on a three-part modular grid, the characters can be read as conventional type or assembled into repeating graphic patterns and visual textures. Designed by Francesco Canovaro and released in 2008, it covers 131 languages across a Latin character set of 204 glyphs. Best suited to posters, experimental layouts and branding where visual rhythm matters as much as readability.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Modulo3 as a squared modular geometric font based on a simple grid of three parts, designed by Francesco Canovaro to explore the intersection of typography and pattern design. Its modular construction allows characters to function as graphic patterns or visual textures, making it suited to experimental projects such as layouts, posters and branding.

name: Quieta
classification_notes: Classified as humanist serif; draws on Italian Renaissance calligraphic tradition with flat-nib stroke construction, angled stress and classical details such as the beaked 'e', balanced by low contrast for text use.
summary: A humanist serif by Maria Chiara Fantini drawing on Italian Renaissance calligraphy and the history of painter Artemisia Gentileschi, spanning six weights with a dedicated Display cut.
description: Flat-nib calligraphic strokes give the letterforms a lively rhythm while keeping contrast low enough for extended text reading, both on screen and in print. Classical details including the beaked 'e' and angled stress of the 'o' sit alongside a wide set of swash, alternate and decorative glyphs for expressive typesetting. A lighter, higher-contrast Display weight handles logos and headlines, and the family covers over 214 Latin languages across 641 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Quieta as a humanist serif inspired by the aesthetics of the Italian Renaissance and the history of Artemisia Gentileschi, the first woman admitted to an Academy of Fine Arts in Italy. Designer Maria Chiara Fantini used sharp flat-nib calligraphic strokes to bring a contemporary energy to traditional humanist proportions, with a dedicated Display weight for headlines and a full range of OpenType features including swash glyphs and small capitals.

name: Amazing Slab
designer: Francesco Canovaro, Mario De Libero, Sofia Bandini, Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: An egyptian-style slab serif with low contrast and the curved endings and open apertures of humanist sans-serifs, making it a humanist slab rather than a mechanical or geometric egyptian.
summary: A humanist slab serif grown out of the Amazing Grotesk family, pairing low-contrast egyptian serifs with open, friendly letterforms across 20 styles including four inline display weights.
description: Developed by Francesco Canovaro and Andrea Tartarelli as a slab companion to Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini's Amazing Grotesk, the design draws its heavier weights from lettering Canovaro originally drew for the StartupItalia logo. The inline variants, drawn by Mario De Libero, extend the family's reach into bold signage, logo work and editorial titling. All 20 styles include true italics, over 900 glyphs, and coverage of 211 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts, with a variable font available in the full package.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Amazing Slab as a typeface that mixes an egyptian serif, low-contrast approach with the curved endings and open shapes of humanist sans-grotesques, developed to embody the energetic and friendly nature of the startup scene. The heavier weights (Medium to Black) each come paired with an inline version designed to extend use into bold signage, logo design and editorial titling, while the lighter weights serve text and display settings.

name: Berton Voyage
classification_notes: Originally hand-drawn by illustrator Matteo Berton for a graphic novel, the typeface uses Didot proportions with chalky, rough handmade textures and randomised glyph sets, placing it firmly in the display/handwritten category rather than conventional serif or script.
summary: A hand-drawn display typeface created by illustrator Matteo Berton for the graphic novel adaptation of Jules Verne's 'Voyage au Centre de la Terre', digitised by Zetafonts with randomised glyph sets to simulate natural handwriting.
description: Originally lettered by Italian illustrator Matteo Berton for La Pasteque's illustrated retelling of the Verne classic, the typeface takes Didot proportions and roughens them with chalky, handmade textures suited to captions and small running text. Three alternate glyph sets rotate via OpenType ligatures to break the mechanical repetition typical of digitised handwriting. Available in two weights with coverage across 190 languages in Latin and basic Cyrillic.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Berton Voyage as the third in their Signature series of artist-designed fonts, hand-drawn by Italian illustrator Matteo Berton and digitised by Zetafonts for the graphic novel 'Voyage au Centre de la Terre'. The typeface uses Didot proportions with handmade irregularities, and employs programmed randomisation of three glyph sets to produce a consistent, realistic handmade appearance across more than 190 languages.

name: Arturo
classification_notes: Arturo draws its forms from brush lettering traditions, giving it the warmth and organic quality associated with humanist sans-serifs, despite its digital construction and versatile text-family structure.
summary: A seven-weight sans-serif family rooted in brush lettering research, with a Heavy Outline style for layered colour font use.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro as part of an investigation into digitally reinventing handmade brush lettering, Arturo balances a lively, playful character with the practicality of a full text family. Seven weights run from Thin to Heavy with matching italics, plus a Heavy Outline variant suited to multilayer colour font work. An extensive OpenType feature set covers small caps, stylistic alternates, unicase, multiple numeral styles and fractions, making it adaptable for both body text and logo design.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Arturo as a sans-serif family designed by Francesco Canovaro emerging from research into the digital reinvention of handmade brush lettering. They highlight its combination of playful letterforms with text-family versatility, seven weights with matching italics, and a broad OpenType feature set including stylistic alternates, small caps, discretionary ligatures and multiple figure styles. The Heavy Outline complements the Heavy weight for multilayer colour font use, and the family supports 205 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts.

name: Codec Warm/Cold
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Francesco Canovaro, Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: A geometric sans rooted in Futura's constructionist logic, offered in two systematic variants: Cold (strict horizontal/vertical terminals) and Warm (diagonal cuts and two-storey forms), placing it firmly in the geometric sans category with a humanist inflection option.
summary: A geometric sans offered in two systematic subfamilies, Codec Cold and Codec Warm, built on the same skeleton but distinguished by terminal treatment and letterform construction.
description: Rather than making arbitrary compromises between geometric rigour and humanist warmth, the designers split the difference into two coherent fonts sharing the same underlying skeleton. Codec Cold cuts terminals parallel or perpendicular to the baseline for a strict, constructed appearance; Codec Warm uses open diagonal cuts and two-storey forms for a gentler texture at text sizes. Both subfamilies span eleven weights from Thin to Fat with matching italics, support 218 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts, and include Logo variants with active discretionary ligatures and tighter metrics for display and identity work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Codec Warm/Cold as a geometric sans serif type system that addresses the tension between geometric perfection and humanist correction by providing two coherent variant fonts on the same base skeleton, rather than a single set of arbitrary compromises. Codec Cold emphasises geometry through strictly horizontal and vertical terminals, while Codec Warm introduces diagonal cuts and two-storey letterforms for a warmer text feel. The family includes small caps, positional numerals, swash forms, over a hundred ligature and alternate glyphs, Logo subfamilies for display and identity use, and four variable font files.

name: Cinematografica
classification_notes: An ultra condensed small caps display typeface designed explicitly for space-saving contexts such as movie posters and screen infographics, placing it firmly in the display category with a highly specialised condensed style.
summary: An ultra condensed small caps display typeface by Francesco Canovaro, originally commissioned for the Lucca Comics 2017 Festival advertising campaign.
description: Built across seven weights from Thin to Heavy, this ultra condensed design prioritises extreme space efficiency, making it well suited to movie posters and screen infographics. OpenType features include alternate glyphs and full-word ligatures triggered by typing an underscore after common words such as 'and', 'or' and 'the'. Script coverage extends to Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 205 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Cinematografica as an ultra condensed small caps typeface developed from Francesco Canovaro's earlier 'Aliens & Cows' project and used in the Lucca Comics 2017 Festival advertising campaign. The family's extreme narrow width is positioned for space-saving applications like movie posters and screen infographics, with OpenType alternates and word ligatures adding expressive flexibility.

name: Kabrio Pro
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: Kabrio is a geometric sans-serif with clean, minimal letterforms and four distinct corner treatments ranging from sharp to rounded, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with display-oriented flexibility.
summary: A geometric sans in four corner-treatment variants — standard, rounded, softer rounded and chamfered — each spanning seven weights with italics, for a total of 56 styles.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Andrea Tartarelli at Zetafonts, Kabrio offers a single underlying geometric sans distributed across four stylistic subfamilies: the standard Roman, the gently rounded Alternate, the more pronounced Soft, and the mechanically chamfered Abarth. Each subfamily runs from Extralight to Heavy with matching italics, giving 56 styles in total. OpenType support covers oldstyle, tabular and fractional numerals alongside stylistic alternates, with Latin and Cyrillic covering 199 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Kabrio as a sans serif for lovers of minimal design, high speed and great curves, likening it to a sports car on the winding roads of the French Riviera. The four corner treatments — standard, alternate, soft and Abarth — offer variation suited to display and logo use, while the extended numeral set makes it equally at home in number-heavy documents such as spreadsheets and timetables.

name: Atlantica
classification_notes: A cursive brushscript drawn from hand lettering, with three optical variants tuned for display, text and signature use. The fluid, connected letterforms and brush-derived strokes place it firmly in the script category.
summary: A cursive brushscript in three variants, drawn by lettering artist Pellisco with Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, tuned for everything from flowing signatures to logotype work.
description: Originating from hand lettering by Pellisco, the family splits into three purpose-built cuts: Signature preserves the raw original letterforms with alternates for display use, Text reins in the lowercase t and makes small legibility adjustments for running copy, and Display goes furthest with automatic OpenType ligatures that knit words into connected lettering compositions. The three styles share 720 glyphs and cover 188 languages across Latin script. Part of Zetafonts' Signature series, which commissions typefaces directly from practising lettering artists.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Atlantica as a contemporary cursive brushscript designed by Pellisco with Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini. It is the third release in their Signature series, which presents typefaces handmade by world-famous lettering artists and illustrators. The Display variant uses extensive OpenType substitutions to turn text into lettering artwork, while the Text variant prioritises readability and the Signature variant stays closest to the original drawn letters.

name: Bulletto
classification_notes: A connecting display script with a slight slant and large x-height, designed explicitly for logos and headlines rather than body text, placing it firmly in the display script category.
summary: A connecting display script from Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, built for logos and headlines with a slight slant, large x-height and an extensive set of OpenType alternates.
description: The family spans five styles across two optical approaches: a slanted connecting script in Light and Regular, an upright Straight variant in the same weights, and Bulletto Alto with taller ascenders and descenders for a more calligraphic feel. OpenType features include standard ligatures, swashes, terminal forms and a full set of stylistic uppercase alternates. Coverage extends to 197 languages across the Latin script with 461 glyphs per style.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Bulletto as a bold display script made for logos and headlines, featuring slight slant, connecting letterforms and a big x-height. The full family includes Regular and Straight cuts with Light companions, plus Bulletto Alto for a more calligraphic look with extended ascenders and descenders. Advanced OpenType features such as swashes, alternate endings and ligatures are noted as requiring compatible software.

name: Banana Yeti
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Andrea Tartarelli, Giulia Ursenna Dorati, Andrea Gaspari
classification_notes: A condensed brush script with a pronounced vertical slant, rooted in a mid-century calligraphic hand. The monoline variant and multiple weights extend the family but the core character is vernacular brush lettering.
summary: A condensed vintage brush script drawn from a mid-century calligraphic sample by Ross Frederic George, expanded into five weights with OpenType swashes and ligatures.
description: Inspired by a handmade lettering sample in the 1947 Speedball Textbook Manual, the design was reworked and extended by the Zetafonts Masterclass 2016 team to add lighter weights and a monoline cut. OpenType features include arching text, ending swashes and a wide set of ligatures that can be triggered by typing underscore sequences, lending a natural, hand-drawn quality. The 728-glyph set covers 187 Latin-based languages and suits food packaging, period advertising and logo work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Banana Yeti as a brush script typeface with a condensed vertical slant, inspired by a calligraphic sample drawn by Ross Frederic George and depicted in the Speedball 1947 Textbook Manual. The original design was completely reworked by the Zetafonts Masterclass 2016 team to add three lighter weights, a monoline variant, and an extended character set with OpenType support for ligatures, alternates, European languages and ending swashes.

name: Beatrix Antiqua
designer: Francesco Canovaro with Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: A humanist sans-serif with variable-width strokes and subtle terminal swelling derived from Roman monumental capitals, placing it in the glyphic or incised tradition akin to Optima rather than a pure grotesque or humanist sans.
summary: A humanist sans whose capitals are drawn from stone carvings in Florence's Santa Croce Cathedral, retaining variable stroke widths and subtle terminal swellings without full serifs.
description: Capitals derived from Florentine monumental inscriptions give the letterforms a glyphic quality reminiscent of Hermann Zapf's Optima, with strokes that taper towards terminals rather than sitting uniformly weighted. The lowercase carries early humanist calligraphic details, most visible in the italics, including a prolonged e nose. Eighteen styles run from Thin to Extrablack with matching italics, and the family covers Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 196 languages.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Beatrix Antiqua as a humanist sans-serif designed by Francesco Canovaro, inspired by Roman monumental capitals and specifically the stone carvings of Florence's Santa Croce Cathedral. Serifs are removed but variable stroke widths are retained, producing a subtle terminal swelling in the same vein as Optima. The lowercase references early humanist typefaces with small calligraphic details, and the family is positioned as a companion to Florentia, with Beatrix Antiqua emphasising the antique stylistic features more strongly.

name: Byom
classification_notes: Byom is built on round, natural shapes with a geometric sensibility, placing it squarely in the geometric sans category. Its emphasis on purity and circular forms is consistent with contemporary geometric sans-serif design.
summary: A round geometric sans in four weights by Francesco Canovaro, released in 2016 and bundled with a pixel-perfect icon set for screen use.
description: Built on circular, natural forms, the family aims for clarity and visual balance across web, app and print contexts. Four weights run from Thin to Bold, supported by broad Latin coverage across 202 languages with a full diacritic set. An accompanying icon set is optimised for 16px and its multiples, making the family well suited to UI work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Byom as a contemporary sans serif by Francesco Canovaro that draws on natural, round shapes to express ease and clarity. The family covers over 40 Latin-script languages with full accents and diacritics, and includes a custom icon set designed for pixel-perfect rendering at 16px screen sizes.

name: Calligraphunk
classification_notes: Calligraphunk simulates polyrhythmic calligraphy through alternating glyph sets, placing it firmly in the script category with an experimental handwritten character rather than a conventional cursive or brush script.
summary: An experimental calligraphic display face that uses alternating glyph sets to mimic the irregular rhythm of handwriting, with a companion mono-width variant for longer text.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini in 2016, the face cycles between two sets of lowercase letters via OpenType substitutions to produce a handwritten feel without manual lettering. The expressive main family suits logo work and headlines, while the mono-width companion brings the same aesthetic to short body copy at more even spacing. Latin extended coverage spans 197 languages across 525 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Calligraphunk as an experimental typeface that mimics polyrhythmic calligraphy by alternating two sets of lowercase letters. They note its powerful flow makes it ideal for logo design and display use, and include a cleaner mono-width variant for longer text blocks, with OpenType features requiring compatible software.

name: Advertising Script
designer: Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini, Matteo Chiti, Luca Chiti, Andrea Tartarelli
classification_notes: A brush script with roots in mid-century American calligraphy, offering multiple weights and a monoline variant typical of the display script category.
summary: A brush script drawn from a 1947 Speedball calligraphy manual sample, reworked into four weights plus a rough variant for vintage display and packaging work.
description: Based on a handmade sample by calligrapher Ross Frederic George, published in the Speedball 1947 Textbook Manual, the family was fully redrawn and extended by the Zetafonts Masterclass 2016 team. Five styles cover Monoline through Bold plus the rough-textured Chupito Brownie cut, with OpenType support for ligatures, contextual alternates and ending swashes accessed via underscore sequences. The 421-glyph set covers 188 Latin-script languages, making it practical for European packaging and period advertising work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Advertising Script as a vintage brush script inspired by a calligraphic sample from the Speedball 1947 Textbook Manual, reworked by the Masterclass 2016 team to add lighter weights, a monoline variant and a rough cut, alongside OpenType ligatures, alternates and swashes, covering over 40 European languages.

name: Jamscript
classification_notes: A handwritten script typeface with flowing letterforms and swash details, typical of casual display scripts used in decorative and seasonal contexts.
summary: A single-weight casual script by Debora Manetti with broad Latin language coverage and a swash character on the underscore.
description: Designed by Debora Manetti and released in 2015 as part of the Christmas Vibes set, this casual script covers 188 languages using the Latin alphabet, with a full set of accents and diacritics across 362 glyphs. A swash character sits on the underscore slot, offering a simple flourish option. Its seasonal origin suggests primary use in greeting, packaging and decorative contexts.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Jamscript as a script typeface designed by Debora Manetti, released as part of the Christmas Vibes set in 2015. It supports over 40 languages using the Latin alphabet, includes a full range of accents and diacritics, and features a swash character accessible via the underscore.

name: Adlibitum
classification_notes: Adlibitum is explicitly described as a contemporary restyling of old textura medieval script characters, placing it firmly in the blackletter/textura tradition, albeit with a geometric and angular modernisation.
summary: A geometric reinterpretation of medieval textura blackletter, built from 45-degree angles and softened corners across three weights.
description: Designed by Francesco Canovaro in 2015, Adlibitum takes the strong vertical rhythm of textura blackletter and recasts it with strict geometric construction, 45-degree angles and soft round corners. Three weights, Light through Bold, cover 197 languages via an extended Latin character set of 407 glyphs. OpenType ligature support and broad diacritic coverage make it serviceable for European-language display work.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Adlibitum as a contemporary restyling of old textura medieval script characters, noting its strong vertical accent and geometric design built on 45-degree angles and soft round corners. It ships in three weights with an extended character set supporting 197 languages and OpenType ligature features.

name: Morbodoni
classification_notes: Morbodoni is derived from the ultrafat Bodoni tradition, a display serif category characterised by extreme stroke contrast. Its rounded corners and softened serifs place it firmly in the fat face display subcategory.
summary: A softened reworking of the ultrafat Bodoni tradition, with rounded corners and reduced contrast for better screen rendering.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini and Francesco Canovaro in 2016, Morbodoni takes the extreme weight of the fat face Bodoni genre and tempers it with rounded terminals and slightly heavier serifs. The result lowers the stroke contrast compared to a conventional ultrafat Bodoni, making it more legible at display sizes on screen. Part of the Fonticio Typeface Family, it covers 197 languages across Latin and Greek scripts.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Morbodoni as a contemporary redesign of ultrafat Bodoni typefaces, featuring rounded corners and slightly fatter serifs to achieve lower contrast and better web rendering. It sits within the Fonticio Typeface Family, intended to deliver a serif with display capabilities alongside reliable text rendering both online and offline.

name: Napo
classification_notes: Napo is explicitly described as a slab serif, designed for both display and body copy use, with sturdy bracketed serifs characteristic of the genre.
summary: A slab serif by Isabella Ahmadzadeh designed for both display and body copy, offered in four weights with italics and broad Latin language coverage.
description: Released in 2016, Napo spans four weights from Light to Extrabold, each with a matching italic, covering 188 languages across the Latin script. It is paired with Leon, a companion sans-serif that shares the same weight range and proportions, giving designers a matched serif and sans within a single purchase. The family is suited to editorial and branding projects that require a slab with reasonable typographic range.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Napo as a slab serif typeface designed for display and body copy use, coming in four weights with italics and supporting over 40 languages using the Latin alphabet. Leon is presented as the companion sans-serif to Napo, available together as part of the full family package.

name: Panforte
classification_notes: A very condensed sans-serif drawn with hand-influenced strokes, intended for readable text setting at narrow widths across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts.
summary: A very condensed sans-serif by Debora Manetti, drawn with loose hand-influenced strokes and optimised for readable text setting across over 200 languages.
description: Designed by Debora Manetti and released in 2013, Panforte is a condensed sans-serif built around big, freely drawn strokes that give it an organic quality while keeping text legible at tight column widths. The family covers 772 glyphs across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts, with OpenType features including small capitals, oldstyle numerals, repeating-letter ligatures and arrow ligatures. Three weights with matching italics make it serviceable for editorial and branding work where space is limited.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Panforte Pro as a prime-cut font family that is deliciously readable online and offline, space-saving and organic. Hand drawn in easy big strokes, it is a very condensed typeface suited to long texts, supporting over 40 Latin-script languages alongside Greek and Cyrillic, and featuring small caps, oldstyle numerals and ligatures for avoiding the repetition of double characters.

name: Handvetica Neue
classification_notes: A sans-serif drawn by hand to mimic the clean geometry of modernist sans-serifs such as Helvetica, giving it a casual yet structured character that sits between display and text use.
summary: A hand-drawn sans-serif by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini that mimics the proportions and structure of classic modernist typefaces like Helvetica.
description: Designed in 2003 and updated as Handvetica Neue, the family offers Light and Regular weights alongside a condensed variant, all drawn by hand to retain an informal quality while echoing the rational geometry of mid-century Swiss sans-serifs. The glyph set covers 205 languages across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts with full diacritics support. It suits branding, editorial and display work where a familiar sans structure needs a human touch.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Handvetica Neue as a hand-drawn sans-serif inspired by classic modernist typefaces, designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini. name: Byron
classification_notes: Byron is categorised as a script face, specifically a handwriting-based font derived from the actual handwriting of Lord Byron, with brush script qualities rather than formal calligraphy.
summary: A single-weight script face by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini based on the handwriting of Lord Byron, released in 2002.
description: Drawn from the actual handwriting of the poet Lord Byron, this script carries an informal, brush-influenced character rather than the formality of classical calligraphic scripts. The family comprises a single style with 170 glyphs covering 114 Latin-based languages including extended European diacritics. foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe Byron as a unique contemporary font that imitates the style of handwriting and brush scripts, created by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini in 2002 from the handwriting of Lord Byron himself.

name: DocPorn
classification_notes: A display typeface designed to mimic hand-drawn comic lettering, with two distinct uppercase sets intended to simulate natural handwriting variation. Falls squarely in the display/comic script tradition.
summary: A comic lettering font with two separate uppercase sets, allowing alternating cases to simulate natural handwriting variation and avoid the doubled-letter problem common to this genre.
description: Designed by Cosimo Lorenzo Pancini in 2004, DocPorn addresses a specific limitation of hand-drawn comic fonts: the unnatural repetition that occurs when the same letterform appears twice in a word. By providing two complete uppercase character sets, users can alternate between uppercase and lowercase to achieve more convincing handwritten rhythm. Coverage extends to 136 languages across Latin script with 212 glyphs total.
foundry_blurb: Zetafonts describe DocPorn as an edgy comic lettering font for grown-ups, positioning it as a mature alternative to Comic Sans and similar hand-drawn fonts that fail with double letters. The two complete uppercase sets allow users to alternate cases for more realistic-looking handwriting.

name: Panforte Serif
classification_notes: A serifed companion to the sans-serif Panforte Pro, sharing the same condensed structure and hand-drawn character; its compact proportions and bold strokes place it in the condensed serif category.
summary: A condensed serif companion to Panforte Pro, designed by Debora Manetti with hand-drawn character and broad multilingual coverage across Latin, Greek and Cyrillic scripts.
description: Released in 2012, this serifed counterpart to Panforte Pro retains the same compact, condensed structure while adding serifs intended to improve readability in extended text settings.
